executive_summary: The Iran-US conflict has escalated to an unprecedented level with Iran launching hypersonic missiles at US forces, targeting the USS Abraham Lincoln with four ballistic missiles, and striking Dubai Airport and Jerusalem's Old City. The death toll from Iranian retaliation has risen significantly with at least 148 killed in an Israeli-US strike on a girls' school in Iran, and nine dead in Jerusalem. The 24h incident count has risen to 573 (200 Major, 240 High), with the conflict now directly involving Cyprus (British bases) and targeting critical Gulf infrastructure.
whats_new: Iran uses Fatah-2 hypersonic missile for first time against US forces (major escalation in weapons capability)
IRGC claims targeting of USS Abraham Lincoln with four ballistic missiles in Arabian Sea
Iranian missiles hit Dubai Airport and Sharjah, causing fires — directly targeting critical UAE infrastructure
Death toll rises to 148 from Israeli-US strike on Iranian girls school (unverified which nation conducted strike)
Iran launches ballistic missiles targeting British military bases in Cyprus — expanding conflict to Western powers
Israeli military official: warhead that hit shelter in Beit Shemesh contained nearly half a ton of explosives
Iranian drone reportedly attacks UAE oil drilling facility — escalating attacks on Gulf energy infrastructure
confirmed_events: Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei killed in US-Israeli strike on Tehran compound — confirmed by multiple sources including BBC, Al Alam, SCMP
Iranian missile strike on Jerusalem's Old City kills nine, causes significant destruction — confirmed by multiple sources
Beit Shemesh missile strike kills eight, injures 27 — confirmed by The Hindu, Al Alam, The New Arab
UAE intercepted 152 of 165 ballistic missiles plus 541 drones since conflict began — Kuwait intercepted 97 ballistic missiles and 283 drones
Former Iranian President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad killed in US-Israeli airstrike on Tehran residence — confirmed by Daily Sabah, Ynet, Jerusalem Post
unverified_emerging: Israeli army denies knowledge of strike on Iranian school killing at least 115 (initially reported as unverified, now death toll at 148 per single source — source: unspecified)
Quds Force Commander Esmail Qaani's disappearance raises concerns — source: unspecified, status unconfirmed
Iranian aircraft attacked UAE oil platform — source: Hezbollah Electronic Army Telegram channel, unverified by mainstream sources
Oil tanker struck by Iranian drones in Strait of Hormuz causing fire — source: Daily Mail, unverified

people_travel: 131 Hong Kong residents stranded in Middle East due to flight cancellations — SCMP
Dubai Airport impacted by Iranian missile strikes causing civilian disruption — confirmed
British PM expresses support for Bahrain amid threats to US naval base
supply_chain: UAE oil infrastructure targeted — Al Salam naval base warehouse struck, oil drilling facility attacked
Strait of Hormuz maritime traffic potentially disrupted by tanker attack
Critical Gulf aviation infrastructure (Dubai, Sharjah airports) directly impacted
market_macro: Oil tanker attack in Strait of Hormuz likely to cause oil price surge — Daily Mail
China calls for cessation of military operations — signaling international market concern
Global supply chain disruption risk rising as conflict expands to Gulf energy infrastructure

outlook_24_72h.base_case: The conflict will continue to escalate over the next 24-72h. Iran has demonstrated willingness to target Gulf states directly (UAE, Bahrain, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia) and has expanded to Cyprus. The use of hypersonic missiles and direct naval targeting (USS Abraham Lincoln) signals Iran is prepared for full-scale military confrontation. With a potential new Supreme Leader election within 48h per Iranian officials, there may be a brief window for internal Iranian deliberation before next phase of operations. Israel and the US are likely to continue intensive strikes on Tehran targeting remaining leadership.
escalation_risks: IRGC naval assets in Gulf may escalate to further target US warships — first direct carrier targeting has occurred
Hezbollah and other proxies likely to increase attacks on Israel as conflict persists
Additional Gulf states could be drawn into direct hostilities — Jordan and Oman already condemned Iranian attacks
Russian or Chinese intervention could shift conflict dynamics — China condemned US-Israel attack
Oil infrastructure attacks could trigger global energy crisis if Strait of Hormuz is further disrupted
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: De-escalation appears highly unlikely in the near term. Iran has stated it will 'vigorously defend its sovereignty' and the IRGC continues announcing new waves of 'Al-Wa'd Al-Sadeq 4' operations. China's calls for ceasefire and Russia's potential mediation remain the most plausible diplomatic channels, though neither side has indicated willingness to negotiate. The killing of Iran's Supreme Leader represents an existential level of conflict that typically does not resolve without complete regime change or military exhaustion.
